problem.title: IMU-DR (pure strapdown INS) aiding ablation on KITTI Raw drive 2011_09_26_0009 (200-frame window, OXTS)
problem.question: How does each cheap aid (ZUPT, midpoint vs Euler integration, static-init gyro-bias estimation) change drift for a pure strapdown IMU-only dead-reckoning baseline on the first 200 frames (~19.9 s) of KITTI Raw drive 0009, using the OXTS-derived imu.csv from evaluation/scripts/kitti_oxts_imu_for_dogfooding.py? Unlike NCLT, KITTI Raw sync OXTS is effectively ~9.7 Hz (one packet per LiDAR frame) and the exporter's imu.csv stamps live on the same synthetic per-frame-index timeline as frame_timestamps.csv (not real seconds) -- both are honest caveats documented below, not tuned around.

dataset.pcd_dir_note: pcd_dir lives on removable media (/media/sasaki/aiueo, external SSD) because this machine's root filesystem had ~16 GB free at evaluation time. GT CSV was independently regenerated from the downloaded KITTI Raw drive and verified byte-identical (md5 match) to the already-committed experiments/reference_data/kitti_raw_0009_200_gt.csv, so no GT changes were needed. If the drive is not mounted, re-export with: python3 evaluation/scripts/kitti_raw_to_benchmark.py --drive-dir <kitti_raw_root>/2011_09_26/2011_09_26_drive_0009_sync --output-dir <ssd path>/dogfooding_results/kitti_raw_0009_200 --gt-csv /tmp/kitti_raw_0009_200_gt_check.csv --max-frames 200 --write-imu-csv (download drive+calib zips from https://s3.eu-central-1.amazonaws.com/avg-kitti/raw_data/2011_09_26_drive_0009/2011_09_26_drive_0009_sync.zip and .../2011_09_26_calib.zip).

variants.label: Default (pure strapdown DR, midpoint, no ZUPT) -- KITTI Raw 0009, 200-frame window
variants.intent: Repository default on the first 200 frames (~19.9 s, 186.97 m trajectory) of KITTI Raw drive 0009: 2.0 s static-init gyro-bias estimation, midpoint integration, ZUPT off. ATE 9769.887 m / RPE 7580.498%/100m (zupt_frames=0), roughly 52x the trajectory length. Two dataset-specific factors make this worse than a naive unaided-DR expectation: (1) the vehicle is already cruising at ~10.7 m/s at frame 0 (min speed over this window: 1.339 m/s, i.e. **never** stationary), so the 2.0 s static-init window is not static; (2) the exporter's imu.csv stamps are spaced 1.0 index-unit apart (harness-reported frame gap: min=median=mean=max=1.000 s), so the strapdown integrator's dt is clamped to max_dt=0.5 s per step vs. the real ~0.103 s inter-frame interval (~4.8x too large per step) -- a structural property of this KITTI-Raw-to-dogfooding fixture (shared by every imu.csv-consuming method here), not something tuned for this run.
variants.note: IMU-DR baseline: unaided strapdown INS, static init 2.0s, midpoint=1 zupt=0 zupt_frames=0; no GT seed.
variants.readability_note: Uses the default CLI surface only.
variants.extensibility_note: No extra profile knobs beyond the stable core contract.
variants.decision_reason: Useful for comparison, but not strong enough to replace the current default.

variants.label: + ZUPT -- KITTI Raw 0009, 200-frame window
variants.intent: Enable zero-velocity updates. ATE 9769.887 -> 214.400 m (-97.81%), RPE 7580.498 -> 172.926%/100m (-97.72%). zupt_frames=174/199 IMU samples (~87.4%) gated stationary -- yet OXTS ground speed **never drops below 1.339 m/s** anywhere in this 200-frame window (verified from oxts vf/vl), so essentially all of these ZUPT triggers are false positives on low-yaw-rate, roughly-constant-velocity cruising, not real stops. The gyro/accel-norm gate cannot distinguish 'stationary' from 'cruising straight at near-constant speed' -- a sharper version of the NCLT full-session caveat (there, ZUPT still corresponded to some genuine low-speed fraction; here it corresponds to essentially none) -- yet it still removes ~98% of the drift by repeatedly re-zeroing velocity error before it compounds through position.
variants.note: IMU-DR baseline: unaided strapdown INS, static init 2.0s, midpoint=1 zupt=1 zupt_frames=174; no GT seed.
variants.readability_note: Adds only boolean toggles on top of the stable CLI.
variants.extensibility_note: Still stays inside the stable CLI, but expands the toggle surface.
variants.decision_reason: Best combined benchmark score on the shared dataset and interface.

variants.label: Forward Euler (vs default midpoint) -- KITTI Raw 0009, 200-frame window
variants.intent: Swap midpoint integration for forward Euler, all else default. ATE 9769.887 -> 9930.599 m (+1.64%), RPE 7580.498 -> 7725.910%/100m (+1.92%). Consistent with NCLT: integration scheme is a small second-order effect next to ZUPT/gyro-bias, even under this dataset's much larger dt/static-init mismatches.
variants.note: IMU-DR baseline: unaided strapdown INS, static init 2.0s, midpoint=0 zupt=0 zupt_frames=0; no GT seed.
variants.readability_note: Adds only boolean toggles on top of the stable CLI.
variants.extensibility_note: Still stays inside the stable CLI, but expands the toggle surface.
variants.decision_reason: Useful for comparison, but not strong enough to replace the current default.

variants.label: No static-init gyro-bias estimation -- KITTI Raw 0009, 200-frame window
variants.intent: Disable the 2.0 s static-init gyro-bias estimate, all else default. ATE 9769.887 -> 1235.339 m (-87.36%), RPE 7580.498 -> 1024.241%/100m (-86.49%) -- an IMPROVEMENT, the opposite ordering from NCLT (where disabling gyro-bias estimation was the single largest degradation, +172%/+183% on the NCLT window). Honest explanation: the static-init window here spans only ~3 IMU samples (stamps 0.5/1.5/2.5 index-units apart, ~0.3 s of real time at ~9.7 Hz) while the vehicle is already moving at ~10.7 m/s -- the 'gyro bias' estimated from 3 samples of a moving, possibly slightly-turning vehicle is contaminated by real angular rate, not sensor bias, so applying it as a bias correction is actively harmful. This is a genuine, reproducible finding on this dataset (same ordering held in the full-session run below), not an aggregation artifact -- and it is the mirror image of the static-init gate's blind spot: the built-in stationarity check only gates on gyro std, so it never flagged that the window was non-static (the vehicle's yaw rate happened to be low during this straight-cruise segment even though its forward velocity was not zero).
variants.note: IMU-DR baseline: unaided strapdown INS, static init 2.0s, midpoint=1 zupt=0 zupt_frames=0; no GT seed.
variants.readability_note: Adds only boolean toggles on top of the stable CLI.
variants.extensibility_note: Still stays inside the stable CLI, but expands the toggle surface.
variants.decision_reason: Useful for comparison, but not strong enough to replace the current default.

variants.label: + NHC -- KITTI Raw 0009, 200-frame window
variants.intent: Enable hard NHC on the KITTI Raw 0009 200-frame window.
variants.note: IMU-DR baseline: unaided strapdown INS, static init 2.0s, midpoint=1 zupt=0 nhc=1 zupt_frames=0; no GT seed.
variants.readability_note: Adds only boolean toggles on top of the stable CLI.
variants.extensibility_note: Still stays inside the stable CLI, but expands the toggle surface.
variants.decision_reason: Useful for comparison, but not strong enough to replace the current default.

variants.label: + NHC + ZUPT -- KITTI Raw 0009, 200-frame window
variants.intent: Combine NHC with ZUPT on the KITTI Raw 0009 200-frame window.
variants.note: IMU-DR baseline: unaided strapdown INS, static init 2.0s, midpoint=1 zupt=1 nhc=1 zupt_frames=174; no GT seed.
variants.readability_note: Adds only boolean toggles on top of the stable CLI.
variants.extensibility_note: Still stays inside the stable CLI, but expands the toggle surface.
variants.decision_reason: Close enough to the current default to keep as a live alternative.

variants.label: + static-init accel-bias estimation -- KITTI Raw 0009, 200-frame window
variants.intent: Estimate accelerometer bias from the static-init window on KITTI Raw 0009.
variants.note: IMU-DR baseline: unaided strapdown INS, static init 2.0s, midpoint=1 zupt=0 nhc=0 zupt_frames=0; no GT seed.
variants.readability_note: Adds only boolean toggles on top of the stable CLI.
variants.extensibility_note: Still stays inside the stable CLI, but expands the toggle surface.
variants.decision_reason: Useful for comparison, but not strong enough to replace the current default.
